recommended_path: accept with documented debt and migration constraints

findings.title: Hero action interaction depth is asymmetric
findings.rationale: Convidar would open a sheet while Compartilhar and WhatsApp remain immediate. The distinction is product-defensible because invite has domain state, but it should be documented to avoid accidental UX drift.
findings.suggested_action: Document the accepted tradeoff: Convidar is modal because it has multiple invite paths and domain state; Compartilhar/WhatsApp are single-intent launchers.

findings.title: Promotion modal migration creates churn risk after approval
findings.rationale: The current promotion modal was manually approved. Moving it to the action-sheet family is still strategically coherent, but requires a migration rationale and regression evidence to avoid gratuitous visual churn.
findings.suggested_action: Migrate promotion only as part of the canonical surface system and require regression evidence that approved web gate behavior remains intact.

findings.title: Shared action sheet may over-abstract distinct contexts
findings.rationale: Promotion, invite first-touch, and share overflow have different data sources and density. A shared component is sound only if it owns anatomy/tokens, not business variant logic.
findings.suggested_action: Define the shared surface as anatomy/layout primitives plus action model; keep business content in feature-owned builders.

findings.title: Full invite composer route must remain explicitly reachable
findings.rationale: Moving from direct route to sheet-first creates a migration risk: the advanced composer could be orphaned if the explicit route action is omitted.
findings.suggested_action: Make the full composer action mandatory in the sheet and test Convidar -> sheet -> full composer navigation.

findings.title: First-touch sheet should defer heavy invite hydration
findings.rationale: The first-touch sheet should remain immediate. Eager loading contacts, inviteable recipients, sent statuses, or contact matching on sheet open would create avoidable latency.
findings.suggested_action: State that the first-touch sheet must not hydrate recipient/contact/status data until the user opens the full composer or a specific action requires it.

findings.title: Test matrix needs concrete navigation and component assertions
findings.rationale: The package lists generic test requirements but does not name deterministic assertions. Implementation could pass shallow tests while missing the canonical sheet-first contract.
findings.suggested_action: Before implementation, define exact widget keys, route assertions, and launcher assertions for Convidar, Compartilhar, WhatsApp, promotion, and full composer reachability.
